title: Exhaust Gas Recirculation Flow Insufficient Detected
shortDescription: EGR system flow is lower than commanded by the PCM.
fullDescription:
The PCM monitors EGR flow through the DPFE sensor or MAP response during EGR activation. When commanded flow falls below threshold, P0401 sets.

Common causes include clogged EGR passages, carbon buildup, or a stuck EGR valve. The code typically appears during steady cruise.
safetyImpact: Increased NOx emissions and potential pinging under load.

fixSteps.title: Scan and confirm
fixSteps.instruction: Read freeze frame, verify P0401 with scanner.

fixSteps.title: Inspect EGR valve
fixSteps.instruction: Remove EGR valve and check for carbon buildup.

fixSteps.title: Clean passages
fixSteps.instruction: Clean EGR ports and intake passages with carb cleaner.

faq.a: Short term yes, but carbon buildup worsens and emissions increase.

title: Exhaust Gas Recirculation Flow Excessive Detected
shortDescription: EGR flow exceeds commanded value, often at idle.
fullDescription:
PCM detects EGR flow when it should be closed or higher than commanded. Usually caused by a stuck-open EGR valve leaking exhaust into intake.

Symptoms include rough idle, stalling, and hesitation.
safetyImpact: Stalling risk in traffic; poor driveability.

fixSteps.title: Test EGR valve
fixSteps.instruction: Check for stuck diaphragm or pintle.

fixSteps.title: Replace if stuck
fixSteps.instruction: Install new EGR valve and gasket.

faq.a: Excess exhaust gas at idle starves cylinders of oxygen.

title: EGR Control Circuit Range/Performance
shortDescription: EGR position feedback out of expected range.
fullDescription:
The PCM monitors EGR valve position and commanded position. A mismatch outside tolerance sets P0404.

Typically caused by a failing position sensor inside the EGR valve or binding pintle.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Verify EGR position sensor signal sweeps 0.5-4.5V.

faq.a: Sometimes, if the pintle is carbon-bound rather than sensor-failed.

title: EGR Sensor A Circuit Low
shortDescription: EGR position sensor A voltage below threshold.
fullDescription: PCM detects EGR sensor A signal voltage below the minimum limit, indicating a short to ground or open circuit.

causes.cause: Short to ground in signal wire

fixSteps.title: Measure signal
fixSteps.instruction: Backprobe sensor connector and check 5V reference and signal.

fixSteps.title: Repair or replace
fixSteps.instruction: Fix wiring or install new EGR valve.

faq.a: On most modern EGRs it is integral to the valve assembly.

title: EGR Sensor B Circuit Low
shortDescription: EGR sensor B signal below expected range.
fullDescription: Vehicles with dual EGR sensors report sensor B low voltage, indicating short to ground, open, or sensor failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install new sensor or EGR assembly.

faq.a: Some DC motor EGRs use two sensors for redundancy.

title: EGR Sensor B Circuit High
shortDescription: EGR sensor B signal above expected range.
fullDescription: PCM detects sensor B voltage above the maximum limit, indicating short to voltage or sensor failure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect harness
fixSteps.instruction: Look for chafing or shorts to 12V.

fixSteps.title: Replace EGR
fixSteps.instruction: Install new valve if sensor faulty.

faq.a: Harness rubbing against bracket near manifold.

title: EGR Sensor A Circuit
shortDescription: General fault in EGR sensor A circuit.
fullDescription: A generic fault in the EGR sensor A circuit not tied to a specific high/low condition. Often intermittent.

fixSteps.title: Wiggle test
fixSteps.instruction: Monitor sensor PID while moving harness.

faq.a: Thermal expansion can open a marginal solder joint.

title: Secondary Air Injection System Malfunction
shortDescription: Secondary air injection system not performing as expected.
fullDescription:
The secondary air injection pump forces air into the exhaust during cold start to reduce emissions. P0410 sets when the PCM cannot verify proper operation.

Causes include failed air pump, stuck check valves, or clogged hoses.
symptoms: Sometimes whirring noise at cold start

fixSteps.title: Command pump
fixSteps.instruction: Use scanner to activate pump and listen.

faq.a: Yes, only cold-start emissions are affected.

title: Secondary Air System Incorrect Flow Detected
shortDescription: Secondary air flow does not match expected value.
fullDescription: PCM measures downstream O2 response or pressure during air injection. Incorrect flow sets P0411.

fixSteps.title: Test flow
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect hose at manifold and verify air pressure.

fixSteps.title: Replace defective part
fixSteps.instruction: Swap pump, valve, or hose as needed.

faq.a: Yes, combi valves fail frequently on N62 engines.

title: Secondary Air Injection System Switching Valve A Circuit Open
shortDescription: Open circuit detected in air switching valve A.
fullDescription: PCM detects no continuity in the switching valve solenoid circuit.

fixSteps.title: Replace valve/wire
fixSteps.instruction: Install new solenoid or repair harness.

faq.a: No, the PCM will keep flagging it.

title: Secondary Air Injection System Switching Valve A Circuit Shorted
shortDescription: Short detected in air switching valve A circuit.
fullDescription: PCM detects a short to ground or power in the switching valve solenoid A.

faq.a: Yes, shorts often blow the associated fuse.

title: Secondary Air Injection System Switching Valve B Circuit Open
shortDescription: Open circuit in switching valve B.
fullDescription: Second switching valve solenoid reports open circuit condition.

faq.a: Yes, just a different solenoid.

title: Secondary Air Injection System Switching Valve B Circuit Shorted
shortDescription: Short in switching valve B circuit.
fullDescription: PCM detects short to ground or power in valve B solenoid.

faq.a: Yes if short not repaired.

title: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 1)
shortDescription: Bank 1 catalytic converter efficiency below minimum.
fullDescription:
The PCM compares upstream and downstream O2 sensor switching. A degraded catalyst lets downstream O2 mirror upstream, setting P0420.

Commonly caused by aged catalyst, engine oil/coolant contamination, or a failing rear O2 sensor.

fixSteps.title: Check O2 waveforms
fixSteps.instruction: Graph upstream vs downstream to confirm cat failure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect exhaust
fixSteps.instruction: Look and listen for leaks before cat.

fixSteps.title: Replace catalyst
fixSteps.instruction: Install new CARB/EPA compliant catalytic converter.

faq.a: May not pass emissions in CARB states; use certified units.

title: Warm Up Catalyst Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 1)
fullDescription: The close-coupled warm-up catalyst on Bank 1 is underperforming during cold start monitor.

fixSteps.title: Inspect exhaust
fixSteps.instruction: Check for leaks and sensor function.

faq.a: A small catalyst close to the manifold that lights off quickly.

title: Heated Catalyst Efficiency Below Threshold
fullDescription: Applies to vehicles with an electrically heated catalyst that fails to reach operating temperature quickly enough.

faq.a: Mostly early SULEV hybrids and some Euro vehicles.

title: Heated Catalyst Temperature Below Threshold
shortDescription: Heated catalyst temp did not reach target.
fullDescription: Catalyst temp sensor reports lower than expected temperature during heated mode.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty part
fixSteps.instruction: Swap sensor or cat as needed.

faq.a: No, only certain hybrid and Euro models.

title: Catalyst Temperature Sensor Circuit (Bank 1)
shortDescription: Fault in catalyst temperature sensor circuit bank 1.
fullDescription: General fault in the bank 1 catalyst temp sensor wiring or sensor itself.

faq.a: Typically threaded into the catalyst body.

title: Catalyst Temperature Sensor Range/Performance (Bank 1)
shortDescription: Catalyst temp sensor reading out of range.
fullDescription: Sensor signal inconsistent with engine conditions, suggesting rationality failure.

fixSteps.title: Compare readings
fixSteps.instruction: Check sensor value vs IR thermometer.

faq.a: Not long-term — OBD readiness will not complete.

title: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 2)
fullDescription: Same as P0420 but on bank 2 (the side opposite cylinder 1 on V engines).

faq.a: Opposite of cylinder 1 — consult service info.

title: Warm Up Catalyst Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 2)
fullDescription: Close-coupled catalyst on bank 2 is not performing during cold-start monitor.

faq.a: P0431 targets the small close-coupled unit, not the main cat.

title: Evaporative Emission Control System Malfunction
fullDescription: PCM cannot verify EVAP system integrity. Often caused by a loose/failing gas cap or leak.
safetyImpact: Fuel vapor leak to atmosphere; emissions fail.

fixSteps.title: Smoke test
fixSteps.instruction: Use EVAP smoke machine to find leak.

faq.a: Yes, cheapest and most common cause.

title: Evaporative Emission Control System Incorrect Purge Flow
shortDescription: EVAP purge flow different than expected.
fullDescription: Purge flow during the EVAP monitor doesn't match expectations, indicating stuck valve or blocked lines.

fixSteps.title: Test purge valve
fixSteps.instruction: Apply vacuum and 12V to verify operation.

faq.a: Yes, stuck-open purge can cause rough idle.

title: Evaporative Emission Control System Leak Detected (Small Leak)
fullDescription: PCM detects a small leak in the sealed EVAP system. Gas cap, hoses, and vent valves are usual suspects.

faq.a: Yes, no drivability impact.

title: Evaporative Emission Control System Purge Control Valve Circuit
shortDescription: Electrical fault in purge control valve circuit.
fullDescription: PCM detects open or short in purge solenoid control circuit.

faq.a: Usually on or near the intake manifold.

title: EVAP Purge Control Valve Circuit Shorted
shortDescription: Short in purge control valve circuit.
fullDescription: Control circuit for purge valve is shorted.

faq.a: Possible — check EVAP fuse.

title: EVAP Vent Control Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: Fault in EVAP vent valve circuit.
fullDescription: Vent valve at the canister is not functioning electrically or mechanically.

fixSteps.title: Replace valve
fixSteps.instruction: Install new vent valve at canister.

faq.a: Stuck closed vent blocks air displacement.

title: EVAP Vent Control Circuit Open
fullDescription: PCM reports open in vent valve circuit.

fixSteps.title: Replace valve
fixSteps.instruction: Install new valve or repair wire.

faq.a: Near charcoal canister under vehicle.

title: EVAP Vent Control Circuit Shorted
fullDescription: Short to ground or power in vent valve control wire.

fixSteps.title: Locate short
fixSteps.instruction: Trace harness visually and with meter.

faq.a: Shorts often blow fuse — check first.

title: EVAP Vent Valve/Solenoid Circuit
fullDescription: Generic fault in the vent solenoid electrical circuit.

faq.a: Yes, it is under the car near canister.

title: EVAP Pressure Sensor Malfunction
fullDescription: Fuel tank pressure sensor signal out of expected range.

fixSteps.title: Read sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Compare FTP value at idle vs ambient.

faq.a: On or near the fuel tank, sometimes on the pump module.

title: EVAP Pressure Sensor Range/Performance
shortDescription: FTP sensor output outside expected range.
fullDescription: Sensor reading inconsistent with driving conditions — rationality fault.

faq.a: Fuel ingress damages the diaphragm.

title: EVAP Pressure Sensor Low Input
fullDescription: Voltage below minimum from FTP sensor.

faq.a: Yes, a clogged reference tube can pull sensor low.

title: EVAP Pressure Sensor High Input
fullDescription: Voltage above maximum from FTP sensor.

faq.a: Yes, corroded grounds at tank are frequent.

title: EVAP Pressure Sensor Intermittent
fullDescription: Signal drops in/out intermittently suggesting loose connector or failing sensor.

faq.a: Often cracked connector housing.

title: EVAP System Leak Detected (Large Leak)
fullDescription: Large leak (~0.090 inch) detected — often an unsealed gas cap, missing o-ring, or disconnected hose.

fixSteps.title: Smoke test
fixSteps.instruction: Use smoke machine to find leak.

faq.a: Large leaks can smell after hot shutoff.

title: EVAP System Leak Detected (Very Small Leak)
fullDescription: Tiny leak detected during cold-soak EVAP monitor. Very hard to find without a smoke machine.

fixSteps.title: Smoke test
fixSteps.instruction: Pressurize system with smoke and inspect.

faq.a: Yes — 0.020 leaks need low-pressure smoke.

title: EVAP Purge Control Valve Circuit Low
shortDescription: Low voltage on purge valve circuit.
fullDescription: Purge control circuit voltage below threshold — short to ground.

faq.a: Not always — often just wiring.

title: EVAP Purge Control Valve Circuit High
shortDescription: High voltage on purge valve circuit.
fullDescription: Control circuit voltage too high — short to voltage or open.

faq.a: Rarely, but check PCM output.

title: Fuel Level Sensor Circuit Malfunction
fullDescription: PCM detects an abnormal fuel level sensor signal. Often a sticking sender float in the tank.
safetyImpact: Running out of fuel due to inaccurate gauge.

fixSteps.title: Replace sender
fixSteps.instruction: Drop tank and replace sender/pump module.

faq.a: Yes but watch trip meter for fuel.

title: Fuel Level Sensor Range/Performance
shortDescription: Fuel level reading doesn't change appropriately.
fullDescription: Level reading fails rationality — doesn't change with fuel consumption.

faq.a: E85 can corrode older senders over time.

title: Fuel Level Sensor Low Input
fullDescription: Signal voltage below minimum — short or failed sender.

faq.a: Yes, low signal = empty indication.

title: Fuel Level Sensor High Input
fullDescription: Voltage above maximum — open circuit or bad sender.

faq.a: Yes but fuel quantity unknown.

title: Fuel Level Sensor Circuit Intermittent
fullDescription: Signal drops in and out, usually a dirty resistor card.

faq.a: Temporarily — best to replace.

title: Purge Flow Sensor Circuit Malfunction
fullDescription: Some vehicles have a dedicated purge flow sensor; this code indicates a circuit issue.

faq.a: No, mostly Ford.

title: Cooling Fan 1 Control Circuit
shortDescription: Fault in cooling fan 1 control circuit.
fullDescription: PCM detects open or short in fan relay 1 circuit. Can cause overheating.

fixSteps.title: Test relay
fixSteps.instruction: Swap fan relay with identical one.

faq.a: No — risk of overheating.

title: Cooling Fan 2 Control Circuit
fullDescription: Same as P0480 but for the secondary cooling fan.

faq.a: Yes, primarily.

title: Cooling Fan 3 Control Circuit
fullDescription: Triple-fan systems (rare) — fault in the third fan control.

faq.a: Certain Euro performance cars.

title: Cooling Fan Power/Ground Circuit
shortDescription: Fan power or ground circuit fault.
fullDescription: PCM detects abnormal voltage at the fan motor supply.

faq.a: Very common on older vehicles.

title: Exhaust Gas Recirculation Throttle Position Sensor Circuit
fullDescription: Diesel vehicles use an EGR throttle to promote flow. This code flags a circuit fault in its position sensor.

faq.a: Mostly yes.

title: Vehicle Speed Sensor Range/Performance
shortDescription: VSS signal out of normal range.
fullDescription: PCM compares VSS to other speed sources (wheel speed, transmission output) and finds an inconsistent signal.

faq.a: Yes — cruise disabled typically.

title: Vehicle Speed Sensor Low Input
fullDescription: Signal voltage below minimum — open circuit or failed sensor.

faq.a: Possible — TCM may default.

title: Vehicle Speed Sensor Intermittent/Erratic/High
fullDescription: Signal drops out or spikes intermittently.

faq.a: Usually replace entire shaft/hub.

title: Idle Air Control System Malfunction
shortDescription: General fault in idle air control system.
fullDescription: PCM cannot maintain target idle speed — IAC valve, throttle body, or vacuum leak related.

fixSteps.title: Replace IAC
fixSteps.instruction: Install new IAC valve if applicable.

faq.a: No — electronic throttle uses different logic.

title: Idle Air Control System RPM Lower Than Expected
fullDescription: Actual idle RPM falls below commanded value.

faq.a: Yes after cleaning TB on ETC systems.

title: Idle Air Control System RPM Higher Than Expected
fullDescription: Actual idle RPM runs high — vacuum leak or throttle not closing fully.

faq.a: Yes, if PCM hunts to close throttle.

title: Engine Oil Pressure Sensor/Switch Circuit
fullDescription: General fault in oil pressure sensor wiring or sensor.
safetyImpact: Unable to detect true low oil pressure — potential engine damage.

fixSteps.title: Verify actual pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Hook manual gauge to confirm safe pressure.

faq.a: Only after confirming real pressure is safe.

title: System Voltage Low
shortDescription: Battery voltage at PCM too low.
fullDescription: PCM monitors its supply voltage. Low voltage indicates charging issue, bad battery, or wiring.

fixSteps.title: Test charging
fixSteps.instruction: Measure battery voltage with engine running (13.8-14.7V).

faq.a: Temporarily — root cause must be addressed.

title: System Voltage High
fullDescription: PCM detects charging voltage above spec — overcharging.

faq.a: Yes — stop driving.

title: Internal Control Module Memory Check Sum Error
fullDescription: PCM's internal ROM/RAM checksum does not match. Often caused by failed programming or internal fault.
safetyImpact: Engine may not start or run correctly.

fixSteps.title: Replace PCM
fixSteps.instruction: Install new module if reflash fails.

faq.a: Rarely — usually needs reflash.

title: Control Module Programming Error
fullDescription: Module is not properly programmed — often after an unfinished flash.

faq.a: Failed flash attempt or dead battery during reprogramming.

title: Internal Control Module Keep Alive Memory (KAM) Error
fullDescription: KAM (keep-alive memory used for adaptations) has failed — usually battery circuit issue.

faq.a: Shouldn't — KAM uses separate circuit.

title: Internal Control Module Random Access Memory (RAM) Error
fullDescription: PCM RAM self-test failed.

fixSteps.title: Replace PCM
fixSteps.instruction: Install new PCM if fault persists.

faq.a: Yes — overheating can damage PCM.

title: Internal Control Module Read Only Memory (ROM) Error
fullDescription: PCM ROM test failed. Usually requires module replacement.

faq.a: No — requires programming.

title: ECM/PCM Processor Fault
fullDescription: Internal processor error — PCM replacement usually required.

faq.a: Sometimes — heat and vibration can trigger.

title: Control Module Performance
shortDescription: Control module performance out of range.
fullDescription: PCM self-test fails rationality check.

faq.a: Yes — always check before condemning PCM.

title: Generator Control Circuit Malfunction
fullDescription: PCM-controlled alternator field circuit has a fault.

faq.a: Yes, check before alternator swap.

title: Generator Lamp Control Circuit
fullDescription: Warning light circuit for charging system faulted.
safetyImpact: May not warn of charging fault.
symptoms: No battery light even when problem exists

faq.a: Yes — on older cars, bulb excites alternator.

title: Generator Field Control Circuit
fullDescription: PCM cannot control alternator field excitation.

fixSteps.title: Test field circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Check PCM command and alternator response.

faq.a: Integrated in most modern alternators.

title: Generator Field Terminal Circuit Low
fullDescription: Field F terminal voltage below expected.

faq.a: Usually yes.

title: Generator Field Terminal Circuit High
fullDescription: F terminal voltage exceeds expected limit.

faq.a: Yes — monitor battery and electronics.

title: ECM/PCM Power Relay Control Circuit Open
shortDescription: PCM power relay control circuit open.
fullDescription: Main PCM power relay control circuit has an open condition.
safetyImpact: Engine may not start or shut down unexpectedly.

faq.a: Yes — common symptom.

title: ECM/PCM Power Relay Sense Circuit High
shortDescription: Power relay sense circuit voltage too high.
fullDescription: PCM detects unexpected voltage on main relay sense line.

faq.a: Briefly after key off — this circuit times that.
